outline.title: Capstone Project
outline.summary: The culminating experience in the Master of Divinity program, where students synthesize theological learning and demonstrate scholarly competency through an independent project.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Course Overview
outline.summary: Capstone Project bridges academic study and vocational ministry, allowing students to apply theological education to real-world contexts or scholarly inquiry with faculty mentorship.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Learning Outcomes
outline.summary: Students will synthesize M.Div. knowledge, conduct rigorous research, develop professional projects, present findings, and reflect critically on theological formation and vocational calling.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Four Project Options
outline.summary: Choose from theological thesis, ministry project, scholarly article, or integrated project combining research, creative work, and practical application.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Course Structure & Requirements
outline.summary: 8-16 weeks duration with 3-6 credit hours, requiring completion of core M.Div. courses and faculty mentorship throughout the project development process.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Assessment Criteria
outline.summary: Projects are evaluated on theological depth, research quality, professional presentation, integration of M.Div. learning, and practical applicability to ministry.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Begin Your Capstone Journey
outline.summary: The Capstone Project represents the culmination of your theological education and the beginning of your professional ministry. Start planning your project today.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed
